approved_answer: Armenia’s health system routes most non-urgent care through the family doctor (therapist/pediatrician) at your local polyclinic. Citizens should first register with a polyclinic by place of residence and choose a primary care doctor . For routine or non-emergency needs (check-ups, chronic care, minor symptoms), always start with your family doctor: they provide basic care and, if needed, refer you to specialists, labs, or hospitals. In contrast, life-threatening or acute emergencies (suspected heart attack, stroke, severe injury, etc.) require an immediate response: call “103” (ambulance) or 112 . Emergency cases do not require a referral.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Ministry of Health (MoH) “Hartak” guides: Official citizen portal summarizing care categories, referral rules, emergency numbers, and registration rules. State Program Services (Hartak): “Receive State-Commissioned Care” pages (En/Hy) instruct citizens to visit their local polyclinic doctor first for referrals . MoH Orders on Referrals and Services (Arlis.am): Ministerial orders (2013–2019) define referral forms and procedures for free care. These establish that primary doctors coordinate referrals, and that emergencies bypass referral requirements. Ministry Communications: Official announcements (e.g.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Routine/non-urgent care: Always go to your registered polyclinic and family doctor first . For example, if you have a mild illness (flu symptoms, chronic disease management, vaccination, etc.), book or walk in to your family doctor or pediatrician at your local health center . They will examine you and either treat you or issue a referral for further care. No need to call the hotline or go straight to a hospital in these cases. Specialist consultation (non-emergency): You cannot (under free care) directly see a specialist without a referral.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Emergency (life- or limb-threatening): Call 103 (ambulance) or 112 immediately. Explain symptoms (chest pain, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, etc.). Emergency services are free for everyone . Alternatively, one can go straight to a hospital’s emergency department. No referral or pre-registration is needed in these cases. Urgent but not immediately life-threatening: If it’s urgent (e.g. severe but stable pain, worsening chronic issue), you may still go to an ER/urgency department (they may triage and treat or admit you). You can also call your polyclinic to ask if next available appointment is soon.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: When referrals are required: Armenia’s official rule is that all state-funded ambulatory care beyond primary doctor visits must be accessed via a formal referral. In other words, to use free or subsidized services (specialist consults, hospital stays, certain diagnostics) you must have a referral issued by a doctor at your polyclinic. This includes specialist appointments and non-urgent hospitalizations. The referral is usually created in the ARMED electronic system by the doctor and can be printed or viewed at the receiving institution. Who issues referrals: Only a qualified physician can issue a valid referral for state-covered care.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Minor or general symptom (“Where should I go?”) → Local Polyclinic, Family Doctor: e.g. mild fever , cough, chronic disease management, check-ups. Use your registered primary care clinic . Need specialist (non-urgent) → Family Doctor → Referral → Specialist: If you think you need a cardiologist, dermatologist, etc., first see your family doctor . They will examine you and, if needed, issue a referral and direct you to the correct specialist clinic or outpatient hospital department. Need lab/image tests (non-urgent) → Family Doctor → Referral: Request a lab test or imaging from your doctor; they will order it.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Emergencies: Any situation posing immediate danger to life or limb qualifies. Examples: suspected heart attack (chest pain), stroke symptoms (sudden paralysis/uneven face), severe breathing difficulties, uncontrolled bleeding, major trauma (falls, accidents, fractures), loss of consciousness, poisoning, etc. In all such cases, call 103/112 or go to the nearest ER. The ambulance is free and its staff will diagnose and stabilize you. There is no need for a doctor’s referral. Urgent care (non-emergency): These are sudden issues that are serious but not immediately life- threatening.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

subtopic: Before you seek care or call for help, gather key information to streamline the process
patterns: Before you seek care or call for help, gather key information to streamline the process
before you seek care or call for help, gather key information to streamline the process
approved_answer: - Identify Service Needed: Know your symptoms/issue clearly (e.g. “knee injury after fall” vs “regular check- up”). This helps direct you to the right provider . - Urgency Level: Decide if it’s an emergency (life-threatening) or routine. If uncertain, err on the side of emergency. - Referral Status: Check if you already have a referral form. Many state-covered services require a referral . If you have one (electronic or paper), have it ready. If not, be prepared to request one from your doctor (unless it’s an emergency). - Registration Info: Know which polyclinic and doctor you’re registered with. If you move or are new, identify the nearest polyclinic for registration.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: “Where do I go for a new health concern?” → Go to your family doctor at your registered polyclinic first . “Do I need a referral to see a specialist?” → Yes, for free/co-paid care a referral from your family doctor is required . • 4 • • • 7 1 7 7 1. 8 1 2. 7 3 4 “I have chest pain: can I call my clinic?” → No – treat as an emergency. Call 103/112 or go to the hospital ER immediately. “I sprained my ankle; where to go?” → If severe (can’t walk), call 103 or visit an ER. If mild, see your polyclinic doctor for advice. “How do I change my family doctor or register?” → Visit any polyclinic in your area with ID; you may choose a doctor within your community. “Can I go to any clinic I want?
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: “Always see your family doctor first.” For non-emergencies, start with your primary care doctor at the local polyclinic. “Referrals unlock care.” An official referral form (usually electronic) is needed for subsidized specialist visits, tests, or hospital care. “Emergency = no referral.” In an urgent life-threatening case, go straight to emergency services (call 103/112) – no referral is required. “Which clinic?” Use the clinic in your region where you are registered. Yerevan residents may choose any city clinic. “Bring ID and docs.” Always carry your ID and any vulnerability/social package cards. Proof of identity/status is needed for free services.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Exact thresholds for “urgent vs emergent.” While examples exist, official triage guidelines (e.g. which pains/symptoms qualify) would help clarify citizen decisions. Direct-access specialists. It is unclear which specialists (if any) might see patients without referrals (e.g. polyclinic ENT or ophthalmology). Clarification of practice vs rule would prevent futile visits. Polyclinic catchment enforcement. The extent to which patients can cross administrative borders for clinics (especially near marz boundaries) is not explicit. Hotline scope. The capabilities and scope of the 8003 hotline (e.g. languages, response protocols) could be better publicized to set expectations. Online services.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Licensing Reforms: The U.S. Commerce Department notes that many health-sector activities require licenses under Armenian law, aligning with the existence of the Law on Licensing. It also points to the e-Gov portal for applications (as above). This confirms the focus on formal licensing processes in healthcare. Doctor Certification (no individual license): Investigative reporting indicates that, unlike some countries, Armenia does not issue individual doctor licenses; instead, doctors receive a certificate of qualification and work under an institution’s license. This validates that our licensing module applies to facilities/practices, not to individual doctors.

subtopic: Group 1: Citizen Support, Rights & Direct Access
patterns: Group 1: Citizen Support, Rights & Direct Access
group 1: citizen support, rights & direct access
approved_answer: Armenia’s Ministry of Health (MOH) maintains a 24/7 public hotline and contact center to assist citizens. All inquiries, complaints or emergency requests can be directed to Hotline 8003 (toll-free) or (+374 60) 80 80 03, and by email at info@moh.am. These services provide personalized guidance (not just automated replies), and if needed the operator will refer cases to appropriate medical or emergency services. The MOH website includes a “Your Rights” section that explains patient protections: the right to informed consent before any treatment, privacy/confidentiality of medical data, and non-discrimination in receiving care.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: The MOH’s central office is organized into divisions such as Public Health, Medical Services, Finance, Legal, International Cooperation, Communications, Human Resources, etc. Each division is responsible for policy development and oversight in its domain. The full organizational chart and list of senior staff is published on the ministry’s website and is regularly updated. Licensing Agency: A specialized body under the MOH that handles licensing of health professionals (doctors, nurses, pharmacists) and health facilities. It issues and renews professional licenses and ensures compliance with education and practice standards.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Application Forms: For permits, licenses, and benefits. For example, downloadable forms to register complaints, request medical records, or apply for State-subsidized care. There are online portals for submitting Freedom of Information requests under Armenia’s transparency law. Regulations and Guidelines: Orders and circulars (indicated by “Order №…”), for example on standard clinical procedures or essential medicines lists. Key laws (Civil Service, Labor , Health Care Law) and regulations (e.g. on laboratory activities, licensing criteria) are accessible in the Legal Acts section.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

subtopic: The MOH actively publishes reports and research to maintain transparency and inform stakeholders
patterns: The MOH actively publishes reports and research to maintain transparency and inform stakeholders
the moh actively publishes reports and research to maintain transparency and inform stakeholders
approved_answer: Annual Reports: The Ministry issues an Annual Health Report detailing system performance (expenditures, service coverage, health indicators). Similarly, the Budget Execution Report is published yearly. These include data on hospital beds, patient admissions, and program costs. Epidemiological and System Studies: Through the National Institute of Health and NCDC, the MOH produces epidemiological bulletins (regular updates on infectious disease trends) and health system analyses (e.g. studies on care quality or resource allocation). These are often presented at conferences or published on the MOH website’s “Research and Analysis” section.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

approved_answer: Armenia has established a Universal Laboratory Network to support diagnosis and surveillance. By law, all public and many private clinical labs must meet quality standards and report results into a central database. The MOH website references the Legal Acts on Laboratory Activities, which cover accreditation, • • • • • 8 6 test reporting and outbreak notification procedures. Specialized labs (e.g. for tuberculosis, HIV testing) are accredited under this system, ensuring nationwide coverage of essential diagnostics. Emergency Medical Services (EMS): The MOH has initiated an Emergency Service Reform to improve ambulance response.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

subtopic: with WHO, EU or US agencies
patterns: with WHO, EU or US agencies
with who, eu or us agencies
approved_answer: Medical and Nursing Education: Regular seminars and workshops are conducted on topics like trauma care, critical care, and infection control. The MOH accredits training institutions and approves curricula updates for medical schools. Nurses and midwives have their own continuing ed programs accredited by the Nursing Chamber , but coordinated with MOH policy. Specialized Programs: Through international grants (e.g. EU Erasmus+ or WHO partnerships), Armenia runs programs to enhance psychiatric training, emergency medicine, and management skills for hospital administrators. For example, joint projects bring in foreign experts to teach new mental health care models or to improve neonatal care.
escalate_if: User asks for a personal case outcome, exact cost, or unsupported exception

Professional Development: Workshops and online courses for healthcare workers on the latest clinical guidelines (e.g. new COVID-19 treatment protocols, stroke care). where do i get a referral

որտեղ գնամ ընդհանուր բուժօգնության ուղեգրի համար
approved_answer: Ճիշտ ուղղորդումը կախված է նրանից՝ ուզում եք դիմել նեղ մասնագետի, հետազոտության, թե ընդհանուր բուժօգնության համար։

field_questions.routing_need_type: Գրե՛ք՝ ինչի համար եք ուզում դիմել՝ նեղ մասնագետի, հետազոտության, թե ընդհանուր բուժօգնության։

answer_rules.answer: Եթե ուզում եք դիմել նեղ մասնագետի, առաջին քայլը սովորաբար պոլիկլինիկա կամ ընտանեկան բժիշկն է, որպեսզի պարզվի՝ տվյալ դեպքում ուղեգիր պահանջվո՞ւմ է, թե ոչ։
answer_rules.next_step: Դիմեք ձեր պոլիկլինիկա կամ ընտանեկան բժշկին՝ տվյալ մասնագետի դիմելու ճիշտ ուղին ճշտելու համար։

answer_rules.answer: Եթե խոսքը հետազոտության մասին է, հաջորդ քայլը կախված է հետազոտության տեսակից և նրանից՝ դրա համար ուղեգիր պահանջվո՞ւմ է, թե ոչ։
answer_rules.next_step: Նախ ճշտեք հետազոտության տեսակը, հետո դիմեք ձեր բժշկին կամ պոլիկլինիկա՝ հասկանալու համար՝ ուղեգիր պետք է, թե կարելի է անցնել անմիջապես։

answer_rules.answer: Եթե հարցը ընդհանուր բուժօգնության մասին է, սովորական առաջին կետը պոլիկլինիկան կամ ընտանեկան բժիշկն է։
answer_rules.next_step: Դիմեք ձեր պոլիկլինիկա կամ ընտանեկան բժշկին՝ հետագա ուղղորդման համար։

safe_partial_answer: Որտեղ դիմելն ուղղակի կախված է նրանից, թե ինչ տեսակի օգնություն կամ ծառայություն է պետք։
next_step_if_missing: Նախ գրե՛ք՝ ինչի համար եք ուզում դիմել։

approved_answer: Եթե ուզում եք դիմել նեղ մասնագետի, նախ պետք է հասկանալ՝ կոնկրետ որ մասնագետի մասին է խոսքը կամ ինչ հարցով եք ցանկանում դիմել։

field_questions.specialist_type_or_purpose: Գրե՛ք՝ որ մասնագետի մոտ եք ուզում գնալ կամ ինչ հարցով է պետք ուղղորդումը։

answer_rules.answer: Նեղ մասնագետի դիմելու դեպքում առաջին հարցը սովորաբար այն է՝ պետք է արդյոք ընտանեկան բժիշկ/պոլիկլինիկա փուլ անցնել, թե տվյալ դեպքում կարելի է դիմել անմիջապես։
answer_rules.next_step: Նախ դիմեք ձեր պոլիկլինիկային կամ բուժող բժշկին՝ ճշտելու համար, թե տվյալ մասնագետի դեպքում ուղեգիր պահանջվո՞ւմ է, թե ոչ։

safe_partial_answer: Եթե հարցը նեղ մասնագետի մասին է, նախ պետք է հստակեցնել՝ կոնկրետ որ մասնագետի կամ ինչ նպատակի համար եք ուզում դիմել։
next_step_if_missing: Նախ գրե՛ք՝ որ մասնագետի մոտ եք ուզում գնալ կամ ինչ հարցով է պետք ուղղորդումը։
